title: AI-Powered Video Editor
description: An AI-powered video editing tool that automatically identifies and removes silent segments from videos, streamlining the editing process for content creators.
markdown:
## What
Chaotics Slice is a self-hosted, AI-powered tool that removes silences from videos. Users upload a video, select an aggression level (Chill → Savage), and the app detects speech while removing all non-speech segments. Timeline previews and exports to fully sliced videos or cut lists (EDL / FCPXML / Premiere XML) enable seamless integration into professional NLEs, offering more precise silence removal than standard volume-threshold trimming.

## How
Built in Python with Flask for frontend and backend. Audio is processed using Silero VAD for frame-accurate speech detection, with cuts applied via FFmpeg through CLI orchestration. Supports macOS, Windows, and Linux with optional GPU acceleration (CUDA / MPS) or CPU fallback. Model weights are cached locally for offline operation after first use. The system automates workflow while retaining precise control over edits.

## Result
A fully functional prototype that accelerates and improves video editing by accurately removing silences, previewing cuts, and exporting directly to NLEs. Self-hosted and free, it allows editors to automate repetitive tasks while maintaining precision and workflow integration.

### AI Usage (80%)
I used AI to develop most of the frontend logic. The command-line MVP using Silero and applying to videos was engineered and prototyped by myself, with gen AI used for code completion.
